rationale: The transferable engine (query re-expressed in hyp_abstract) is degree-based mean-field theory on a random network: derive a critical threshold from moments of the degree distribution P(k), which vanishes/collapses when the second moment diverges (fat-tailed/scale-free P(k)), producing anomalously smooth, size-sensitive critical behavior. The candidate applies this exact machinery to rumour spreading — mean-field equations on random graphs vs. scale-free networks, explicitly finding the spreading-rate threshold becomes "vanishingly small in the limit of infinite system size" for scale-free topology, the same moment-driven threshold-collapse phenomenon, so method_coherence is TRUE. The object is genuinely different: the query studies an Ising ferromagnet (spins on a graph), while the candidate studies rumour/information diffusion among individuals in social networks — a different substrate and phenomenon (social contagion dynamics vs. magnetic statistical mechanics) — so object_difference is TRUE.
